revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Akuto-jinja]][[ja:阿久刀神社]] {{Expand Japanese|阿久刀神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Akuto Shrine | native_name = 阿久刀神社 | image = Akuto-jinja shaden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Akuto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.855334|135.603167}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|de|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|it|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|mni|ꯁꯨꯃꯤꯌꯣꯁꯤ ꯁꯟꯖꯤꯟ|my|ဆုမိရောရှိနတ်သုံးပါး|pl|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|simple|Sumiyoshi sanjin|zh|住吉三神|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} }} {{nihongo|'''Akuto Shrine'''|阿久刀神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi shrine|de|Sumiyoshi-Schrein|fr|Sanctuaire Sumiyoshi|qid=Q32512|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11498550|ltq=Q11498550|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 20 ** P155: {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|fr|Ikasuri-jinja|ja|坐摩神社|simple|Ikasuri Shrine|zh|坐摩神社|qid=Q11425667|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Nomi Shrine|lt=Nomi Shrine|ja|野見神社 (高槻市)|qid=Q11646130|1=Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q335423|ltq=Q335423|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} * {{ill|Settsu Province|lt=Settsu Province|ca|Província de Settsu|cs|Provincie Seccu|de|Provinz Settsu|en|Settsu Province|eo|Provinco Seccu|es|Provincia de Settsu|fa|ولایت ستسو|fr|Province de Settsu|gl|Provincia de Settsu|hak|Settsu-koet|id|Provinsi Settsu|it|Settsu (provincia)|ja|摂津国|ko|셋쓰국|lt|Seccu žemė|nl|Settsu (provincie)|pt|Província de Settsu|ru|Сэтцу (провинция)|simple|Settsu Province|sk|Seccu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Q10965808|ltq=Q10965808|qid=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.855334|135.603167}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Akuto-jinja shaden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|de|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|it|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|mni|ꯁꯨꯃꯤꯌꯣꯁꯤ ꯁꯟꯖꯤꯟ|my|ဆုမိရောရှိနတ်သုံးပါး|pl|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|simple|Sumiyoshi sanjin|zh|住吉三神|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181040 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * Мастор|my|ဂျပန်နိုင်ငံ|mzn|جاپون|nap|Giappone|nds_nl|Japan|nds|Japan|ne|जापान|new|जापान|nia|Jepang|nl|Japan|nlnews|Categorie:Japan|nlvoyage|Japan|nn|Japan|nov|Japan|no|Japan|nqo|ߗ߭ߊߔߐ߲߫|nrm|Japon|nr|Japane|nso|Japane|nv|Binááʼádaałtsʼózí Dineʼé Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Akuta River|lt=Akuta River|ja|芥川 (大阪府)|qid=Q11614729|1=Akuta River}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 8126339 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7120905002031 == Commons category (P373) == * Akuto-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府高槻市清福寺町23-2', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Akuto-jinja torii.JPG {{commons category|Category:Akuto-jinja}} [[JA:阿久刀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11657135}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Akuto Shrine |Image = [[File:Akuto-jinja shaden.JPG|260px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Seifukuji-chō 23-2 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Rituals = Kagura gathering; Yutate ritual (second Sunday of April) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Akuto Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Akuto-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Akuto Shrine''' (あくとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Seifukuji-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}Shrine origin document.“Akuto Shrine,” Takatsuki City Board of Education, March 2015 (on-site information board). ** A collective term for Sokotsutsu-no-Ōno-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-Ōno-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-Ōno-Mikoto. Because it is commonly called “Sumiyoshi-san,” the enshrined deity is listed as the Sumiyoshi deity in the *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmei-chō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry for Akuto Shrine.. However, *Setsuyō Gundan* identifies it as a Suwa deity, while other theories propose Akutohime (consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|qid=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}), or a deity named Kudo{{Cite web|ja|year=2012 |url= http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/daionokuni/1327734538892.html |title=Akuto Shrine |work=Internet History Museum |publisher={{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} |accessdate=2015-08-07}}. === History === === Founding === The founding date is unknown, and several theories have been discussed. *Shinmyōchō Kōshō* argues a connection with “Akuta,” who is mentioned in the “Tsuki no Muraji” entry of the *{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}*“Shinsen Shōjiroku,” Sakyo Shohan, Tsuki no Muraji entry., as someone who presented silk to {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|qid=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}. It further notes that Tsuki no Muraji are recorded as descendants of {{ill|lt=Nori no Omi|Q119380486|qid=Q119380486|1=Nuri no Omi}}, identifying him with “Nuri no Mi,” who appears in the *{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}* as presenting silkworms to the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. From this, it is inferred that Akuta, an immigrant with sericulture technology, lived in this area and enshrined his clan deity here. Another theory points to the “{{ill|lt=Ato clan|Q17191532|qid=Q17191532|lt=Ato no Muraji|1=Ato no Muraji}},” descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}, recorded in the Settsu Province section of the *Shinsen Shōjiroku*“Shinsen Shōjiroku,” Settsu Province, Shinbetsu, Ato no Muraji entry., suggesting that the shrine name derives from this clan. It is also said that the name of the {{ill|lt=Akutagawa River (Osaka)|Q11614729|qid=Q11614729|1=Akuta River}} flowing north of the shrine precincts derives from the shrine’s name, changing from “Akutogawa” to “Akutagawa” over time{{Cite book|ja |editor=Haruo Shimomura (ed.) |year=2005 |title=Historical Walk Along the Saigoku Kaidō |publisher=Kansai Historical Walk Association |page=187}}. === Overview === The *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}*, compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}} during the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, lists “Akuto Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimagami District|Q10965808|qid=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, Settsu Province,” and ranks it as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Although its subsequent history is unclear, the shrine was burned down during the wars involving the {{ill|lt=Miyoshi clan|Q2975329|qid=Q2975329|1=Miyoshi clan}} and {{ill|lt=Matsunaga clan|Q11531583|qid=Q11531583|1=Matsunaga clan}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era (1558–1570). The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1828|Q7570|qid=Q7570|1=1828}} (11th year of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|qid=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}}). During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was known as “Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} (5th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}), during shrine land reorganization, nearby Suwa Shrine and Daishōgun Shrine were relocated to become auxiliary shrines within the precincts. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === The sacred {{ill|lt=Mukunoki tree|Q4779482|qid=Q4779482|1=Aphananthe aspera}} standing in the precincts is 21.5 meters tall with a trunk circumference of 3.45 meters{{Cite web|ja|author=Hideki Kikuchi |url= http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/82/hogojumokuichiran1-2.pdf |format=PDF |title=Protected Trees (List of Protected Trees 1) |work=Designation of Protected Areas (2) Protected Trees |publisher={{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} |accessdate=2015-08-08}}. It was designated a municipal protected tree in {{ill|lt=1990|Q2064|qid=Q2064|1=1990}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}). File:Akuto-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Akuto-jinja shinboku.JPG|Sacred mukunoki tree === Auxiliary shrines === All are within the precincts. * Daishōgun Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Suwa Shrine * Kojima Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Daishōgun Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival (1st of every month, 10:00 a.m.)Festival information according to the shrine origin document. * New Year’s festival; Great torii chinowa purification ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}}–{{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|qid=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Tondo festival ({{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|qid=Q2260|1=January 15}}) * Yakuyoke Setsubun festival ({{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|qid=Q2313|1=February 2}}) * Doll burning ritual ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|qid=Q2315|1=February 3}}) * Spring festival; yutate ritual (second Sunday of April) * Autumn festival (October; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shichi-Go-San visit; wisdom-ring purification ({{ill|lt=November 15|Q2982|qid=Q2982|1=November 15}}) === Access === * From the north exit of {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q1071995|qid=Q1071995|1=Takatsuki Station (Osaka)}} on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West), take the {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City Bus|Q11670759|qid=Q11670759|1=Takatsuki City Bus}} bound for Kansai University and get off at “Gunge Hongō” bus stop; 3-minute walk. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture* ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry for Akuto Shrine === External links === {{Commonscat|Akuto-jinja}} * [http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/daionokuni/1327734538892.html Akuto Shrine] – Takatsuki City Internet History Museum * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_16_takatuki_akuto.html Akuto Shrine] – Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Akuto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Akuto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ato clan]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ato clan]]
